purpose.summary: This copybook defines the structure of the ERROR-LOG-RECORD, which is used for logging errors and informational messages within an application. It includes fields for date, time, application name, program name, location, severity level, subsystem, error codes, message text, and an event key.

paragraphs.purpose: This copybook defines the ERROR-LOG-RECORD data structure, which serves as a template for logging error and informational messages. The record includes fields for capturing the date (ERR-DATE) and time (ERR-TIME) of the event, the application (ERR-APPLICATION) and program (ERR-PROGRAM) where the event occurred, and a location code (ERR-LOCATION). It also specifies the severity level (ERR-LEVEL) using condition names like ERR-LOG, ERR-INFO, ERR-WARNING, and ERR-CRITICAL, as well as the subsystem (ERR-SUBSYSTEM) using condition names like ERR-APP, ERR-CICS, ERR-IMS, ERR-DB2, ERR-MQ, and ERR-FILE. The record further includes two error code fields (ERR-CODE-1 and ERR-CODE-2), a message field (ERR-MESSAGE) to store the error description, and an event key (ERR-EVENT-KEY) for tracking purposes. This structure is intended to provide a standardized format for error logging across different applications and subsystems, facilitating easier analysis and troubleshooting.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'ERROR-LOG-RECORD' is never used by any program

open_questions.question: How is the ERR-LOCATION field populated and what does it represent?
open_questions.context: The purpose of the ERR-LOCATION field is unclear from the copybook definition.
open_questions.suggestion: Review the programs that use this copybook to understand how the ERR-LOCATION field is used.

dead_code.name: ERROR-LOG-RECORD
dead_code.reason: Record layout 'ERROR-LOG-RECORD' is never used by any program
